tool.name: aaindexextract
tool.summary: Report one governed built-in amino-acid property table

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `aaindexextract` exposes a governed built-in subset of amino-acid property indices. The EMBOSS-RS v1 implementation is intentionally narrower than historical EMBOSS and does not claim full AAINDEX database coverage. It emits one deterministic residue table for a requested built-in index.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one required built-in index name and no sequence input. Supported index names are `hydropathy_kyte_doolittle`, `average_mass`, `charge_class`, and `polarity_class`. Short aliases `hydropathy`, `mass`, `charge`, and `polarity` are also accepted.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits a stable table report with columns `index`, `residue`, `three_letter`, `name`, `value`, `units`, and `notes`. Rows preserve a fixed canonical amino-acid order from `A` through `V`.

sections.title: Subset Model
sections.content: Hydropathy scores use the same Kyte-Doolittle table used by `pepwindow`. Average masses use the same residue-mass table used by `pepstats`. `charge_class` and `polarity_class` are reported as coarse categorical classes rather than as historical AAINDEX numeric scales.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs aaindexextract`. Validation currently covers supported built-in index parsing, stable residue-table emission, and service-level reporting through the shared statistics path.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not ingest external AAINDEX files or expose the broader historical index catalogue. It is a governed built-in subset only.

artifacts.label: AAindexextract hydropathy case note
artifacts.description: Repository-managed case note describing the governed built-in hydropathy subset example.

examples.title: Report the built-in hydropathy subset
examples.description: Emits the stable amino-acid property table for the governed hydropathy subset.

expected_outputs.label: Built-in amino-acid property table
expected_outputs.description: A stable residue table for the requested built-in index.
